hard.next_step: Small update as I am in Struer today and have talked to finance. As finance are swamped in the year end activities June/May, we have pushed next step to later in June.
hard.ais_why: X7_Details_about_historical_migration captures a buyer-stated AI need verbatim: 'Yes need AI for invoice extractions and Intake.' Scope includes Merlin alongside eProcurement and eInvoicing. Capped below 8 because BD_Engagement is 'Unengaged', no upcoming meetings logged, and stage-slip and close-date-push flags are active.
hard.competitor: Two unnamed shortlisted P2P vendors (Zycus down-selected to 3, 13 Apr 2026); Microsoft Dynamics (future ERP direction); incumbent SAP ECC 'Bear Shop' being decommissioned
hard.primary_competitor: Two unnamed shortlisted P2P vendors (final field of 3)

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy Shortlisted P2P deal with a strong champion, but momentum is thinning: the finance business case is gated by year-end, the economic buyer is still unengaged, and entry scope was already trimmed from 100k to 76k.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 25 Nov 2025, Shortlisted since 09 Dec 2025 (~6.9 months in stage as of 06 Jul 2026). Close 30 Nov 2026, ~147 days out after a 29 May push from 31 Jul 2026. Last activity 23 Jun 2026 (business-case tasks logged). The two 2026 syncs (17 Mar, 27 Apr) had note-takers turned off, so no fresh transcribed buyer content this run. The live gate is the finance-owned business case for internal approval (task 23 Jun, due 30 Jun, overdue), stalled while finance clears year-end; the deal is alive and Jonas is engaged, but the key advancing action is waiting on finance and the economic buyer has never been engaged.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline. Best Case is aggressive at Shortlisted with the economic buyer unengaged, no pricing sign-off, the business case not yet delivered, and the close date freshly pushed four months. Defensible category is Pipeline until the finance business case lands and a dated decision step with the budget owner is confirmed.
evidence: Next_Step (16 Jun 2026, Casper Hoeholt): finance swamped in year-end activities June/May, next step pushed to later in June.
Task 23 Jun 2026 (Casper Hoeholt): 'Finance (Bang & Olufsen) to deliver business case for internal approval' - due 30 Jun 2026, status overdue.
Field history 29 May 2026: CloseDate 2026-07-31 -> 2026-11-30 (four-month push, by Casper Hoeholt).
Field history 09 Dec 2025: Amount 100000 -> 76000 at the Formal Evaluation -> Shortlisted transition (entry scope trimmed).

metrics.narrative: The pain is clear but never quantified by the buyer in a dated figure. Jonas Elber described the incumbent 'Bear Shop' custom app on SAP ECC as having 'a lot of errors, a lot of limitations, and it cannot support dual ERPs' (Avoma alignment call, 20 Nov 2025), and the buyer-stated AI need is verbatim 'need AI for invoice extractions and Intake' (SF X7 migration field). No cost, headcount, invoice-volume, or cycle-time number has been attached to that pain, which is exactly why finance is being asked to build the business case. Quantifying the invoice-processing savings with finance is the ranked move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named or engaged after ~7 months. The evidence points to finance leadership owning the decision: the 23 Jun 2026 tasks explicitly route the business case to 'Finance (Bang & Olufsen) to deliver business case for internal approval', and Casper's 16 Jun note describes talking to finance in Struer, gated by year-end workload. So budget authority sits with an unnamed finance/CFO owner reached only indirectly through Jonas and Casper's on-site visit. Getting Jonas to name and introduce that finance approver is the highest-leverage MEDDPICC move.
sources: Task 23 Jun 2026 (Finance to deliver business case)

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are known from the 2025 demo burst but not re-confirmed on a 2026 transcribed call. The buyer weighted dual-ERP support (running SAP ECC alongside a likely future Microsoft Dynamics move), AI-driven invoice extraction and intake (Merlin), and replacement of the failing Bear Shop app (Jonas Elber, Avoma alignment 20 Nov 2025). P2P scope was approved and Zycus was down-selected to a field of three (SF Next_Step_History 13 Apr 2026), which confirms Zycus meets the shortlist bar. What is not documented is the finance-side commercial criteria that will decide the business case.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a technology-led shortlist heading into a finance-gated approval. Jonas Elber, promoted to Head of Technology, drove the demos, holds the new proposal, and is preparing the business case (SF Next_Step_History 29 May 2026); the last steps recorded are finance building the internal business case for approval (Task 23 Jun 2026). The approval chain therefore runs Jonas -> finance business-case sign-off -> budget release, but the finance approver and any IT/digital board step are not yet named or dated. The two 2026 syncs were held with note-takers off, so the process detail was not captured this run.
sources: Avoma syncs 17 Mar and 27 Apr 2026 (note-taker off)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible: no MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal, procurement, or InfoSec step has surfaced in the record, which is normal at Shortlisted before commercials are locked. This is a new-business P2P deal (eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin) so it will eventually need MSA plus Order Form and a SOW. Visibility will come once the finance business case is approved and pricing is agreed; asking Jonas to outline B&O's procurement and legal path is what would create it.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is well established and owned by Jonas Elber (Head of Technology). The custom Bear Shop app on aging SAP ECC 'has a lot of errors, a lot of limitations, and it cannot support dual ERPs' and 'will be decommissioned during next year' (Jonas Elber, Avoma alignment 20 Nov 2025), and B&O manages procurement without modern P2P automation. The buyer articulated a specific AI pain: they 'need AI for invoice extractions and Intake' (SF X7 migration field). The pain is confirmed and current; what is missing is the quantified cost of that pain for the business case.

champion.narrative: Jonas Elber is a genuine, recently strengthened champion: he is the primary Salesforce contact (role Decision Maker, Head of Technology, jone@bang-olufsen.dk), drove every demo, was promoted to Head of Technology, and is personally holding the new proposal and preparing the business case (SF Next_Step_History 29 May 2026). His advocacy is strong on the technology side. The single limitation is reach into power: he has not yet pulled the finance economic buyer into the deal, so the deal remains single-threaded through him. Securing his introduction to finance is the way to convert a strong technical champion into an approved business case.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Jonas Elber, Decision Maker, primary)

competition.narrative: Zycus is a live finalist: P2P scope approved and down-selected to three vendors (SF Next_Step_History 13 Apr 2026), but the two co-finalists have never been named. The incumbent Bear Shop app on SAP ECC is being decommissioned and is not a threat, and Microsoft Dynamics is only a possible future ERP direction, not a P2P bidder. Zycus's wedge against the unnamed finalists is Merlin AI invoice extraction and intake (the buyer's own stated need) plus dual-ERP support. Identifying the two rivals through Jonas is required before the field can be ranked; no fresh competitive evidence surfaced this run because both 2026 syncs were held with note-takers off.

deal_movement.summary: Steady-but-slowing pulse: after a strong Oct-Nov 2025 demo burst and a spring 2026 shortlist win, the last two syncs (17 Mar, 27 Apr) were held with note-takers off and the deal now waits on a finance business case that is gated by year-end and overdue.

items.change: Finance (B&O) tasked to deliver the business case for internal approval; due 30 Jun, now overdue
Casper visited Struer and spoke to finance; next step pushed to later in June as finance clears year-end
Close date pushed 31 Jul 2026 -> 30 Nov 2026 (four-month slip)
Champion Jonas Elber promoted to Head of Technology and preparing the business case from the new proposal
P2P approved; Zycus shortlisted between 3 vendors; Anthony to align with Jonas on pricing

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is a finalist in a down-selected field of three but the two co-finalists remain unnamed, which is the single most important competitive gap. As of 13 Apr 2026 Zycus is shortlisted for P2P; the incumbent 'Bear Shop' custom app on SAP ECC is being decommissioned (not a threat) and Microsoft Dynamics is only a possible future ERP direction. The strongest current threat is whichever of the two unnamed co-finalists is still live; Zycus's edge is Merlin AI invoice extraction/intake (the buyer's stated need) plus dual-ERP support. No new competitive evidence this run (both 2026 syncs had note-takers off), so threat levels are carried forward, not re-ranked.

competitors.name: Two unnamed shortlisted vendors (P2P final field)
competitors.quote: P2P approved. Zycus Shortlisted between 3 vendors. Anthony to align with Jonas around pricing.
competitors.how_we_win: Get Jonas to name the two rivals, then anchor on Merlin AI invoice extraction/intake and dual-ERP handling where they are weakest.

competitors.name: Microsoft Dynamics (future ERP direction)
competitors.quote: I would be surprised if we don't go Microsoft way, but of course there needs to be a decision on this.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as ERP-agnostic: it runs across SAP ECC and a future Dynamics move, so choosing Zycus does not require re-platforming procurement.

competitors.name: SAP ECC 'Bear Shop' (incumbent custom P2P app, being decommissioned)
competitors.quote: It is an application that has a lot of errors and limitations, and it cannot support dual ERP solutions. So this application will be decommissioned during next year.
competitors.how_we_win: Incumbent is already being retired; use its errors, no dual-ERP support, and free-text spend as the baseline pain in the business case.

competitors.name: Unknown SharePoint-layer CLM vendor (parallel CLM buying-group context)
competitors.quote: I can be very transparent with you, we are looking into a solution that sits on top of SharePoint today.
competitors.how_we_win: Carried from the parallel CLM thread; on P2P, keep the wedge on AI-native invoice intake versus a repository overlay.

competitors.name: SharePoint (incumbent CLM repository, parallel context)
competitors.quote: 10,000 contracts stored in SharePoint, difficult to manage (Jonas Elber, 21 Nov 2025 CLM demo)
competitors.how_we_win: Parallel CLM context; not a P2P competitor. Zycus adds the intelligence and process layer on top of storage.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is well aligned to B&O's stated technical needs (dual-ERP, AI invoice intake, replacing the failing incumbent) but not yet proven against finance's commercial expectations, which is where the deal now sits.

items.criterion: Dual-ERP support (SAP ECC now, possible Microsoft Dynamics later)

items.criterion: AI for invoice extraction and intake (Merlin)

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is single-threaded through Jonas with a defensive scope cut and an unengaged budget owner; the win levers are multi-threading into finance, protecting scope, and naming the rivals.
flags: Multi-thread into finance now: the deal runs entirely through Jonas and the budget owner has never been engaged, so ask him to introduce the finance approver to co-present the business case.
Protect scope: entry value was cut from 100k to 76k on 09 Dec 2025, a defensive signal, so reconfirm the P2P scope and offer a phased path rather than let it erode further.
Name and rank the two unnamed P2P co-finalists via Jonas, then anchor the Merlin invoice-intake and dual-ERP wedge where they are weakest.

items.title: Head of Technology
items.risk: Deal is single-threaded through him; strong technical advocate and primary decision maker, but has not yet pulled the finance economic buyer in. If he is diverted by year-end IT work, momentum stalls.

champion_strength.summary: Jonas Elber is a strong, recently strengthened champion: primary Salesforce decision-maker contact, promoted to Head of Technology, drove every demo, and is personally preparing the business case from the new proposal. The gap is that his advocacy has not yet reached the finance economic buyer, so the deal remains single-threaded.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin AI invoice extraction and intake maps directly to the buyer's own stated need, so Zycus's AI story is on target; the risk is that the AI value has not been quantified for finance, so make Merlin invoice-intake ROI the centerpiece of the business case.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7 (Moderate). The buyer originated the AI language ('need AI for invoice extractions and Intake') and Merlin is in scope, but only one AI module is in play and BD_Engagement reads 'Unengaged', so appetite is real but not yet a driving force. Calls this run were note-taker-off, so no fresh AI signal to re-rate.

scope_change.detail: Amount cut ~24% from 100k to 76k on 09 Dec 2025 at the Formal Evaluation -> Shortlisted transition, framed as 'good scope for entry point in P2P'. A defensive narrowing consistent with a cost-conscious, phased-entry buyer; protect scope and offer a phased path rather than let it erode further.

customer_preference.evidence: Jonas rated Zycus's AI ahead of competition and drove every demo (Nov 2025), and Zycus was down-selected to the final 3 (13 Apr 2026); no fresh 2026 preference signal since both syncs were note-taker-off.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain is clear (failing Bear Shop app, need AI for invoice extraction/intake) but no ROI or savings figure quantified; finance is only now building the business case (Task 23 Jun 2026).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is a shortlisted P2P finalist (down-selected to 3) carried by a strong, promoted champion in Jonas Elber, but the win hangs on an overdue finance business case and an economic buyer who has never been engaged; entry scope was already trimmed from 100k to 76k.

win_position.text: Sits around 56 against a Shortlisted cap near 70: strong champion and clean technical fit earn the shortlist, but we hold it down because the economic buyer is unengaged, the business case is overdue, and scope was already cut.

Jonas Elber, promoted to Head of Technology and the primary Salesforce decision-maker, drove every demo, rated Zycus's AI ahead of rivals, and is personally preparing the business case (Next_Step_History, 29 May 2026).
The finance economic buyer has never been named or engaged after ~7 months, so the deal is single-threaded through Jonas and the budget sign-off is unowned (Task, 23 Jun 2026).
Entry value was cut from 100k to 76k on 09 Dec 2025, a defensive scope signal, and the business-case task has been overdue since 30 Jun with no fresh transcribed buyer engagement in 2026.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is thinning: the last two syncs (17 Mar, 27 Apr) were held with note-takers off and the close date was pushed four months to 30 Nov 2026 on 29 May.
The advancing action, finance's internal business case, is gated by year-end and overdue since 30 Jun 2026 (Task, 23 Jun 2026).
Commercial topics are live (pricing alignment tasked to Anthony, 13 Apr; finance business case in progress), so the deal is moving into the money conversation.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer approved P2P scope and down-selected Zycus into a field of three, a real commitment of evaluation effort (Next_Step_History, 13 Apr 2026).
Commitment is capped by the trimmed 76k entry scope and the absence of any customer-named decision date or requested next meeting.

deal_risk.text: Single-thread through Jonas with the finance budget owner unmapped is the top risk to converting the shortlist into a signed deal.
Repeated close-date pushes (31 Jul -> 30 Nov) plus a defensive scope cut point to a cost-conscious buyer that could stall or phase the project further.
